of compliance costs associated with this.

There are some opportunities to make some real improvements

both the providers

Reporting on outcomes is critical to public accountability
as they reflect what governments are trying, uh, are seeking
to achieve, uh,
and how people think about what it is the government does.
The performance reporting system
doesn't do this well at the moment.
This reflects, uh, well-known challenges
with outcome reporting.
So outcomes reflect the actions of multiple actors inside
and outside government over a number of years.
This makes it difficult to attribute government action
to achievement of outcomes
and to assign accountability to those.
While there might be broad agreement on what outcomes
to achieve, there are often legitimate differences on
how best to achieve those outcomes.
In these cases, scrutiny
of an agency's output delivery can
become a political debate.
We nevertheless urge you to focus on
how this can be done in a meaningful way.
If we are to improve outcome reporting.

public sector agencies
